Search the FormalConjectures directory for any existing Lean code that deals with similar mathematical concepts to what might appear in Erdos Problem 185. Look for:
1. Files dealing with sumsets, subset sums, or additive combinatorics
2. Files dealing with density of sets of natural numbers or integers
3. Any utility definitions related to sumsets, asymptotic density, or Schnirelmann density
4. Check FormalConjecturesForMathlib/ directory for any reusable definitions

Be thorough - search for keywords like "sumset", "density", "Schnirelmann", "additive", "basis", "representation", "partition", "sum", "subset_sum" across .lean files. Report what you find.

    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 185
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/185](https://www.erdosproblems.com/185)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $f_3(n)$ be the maximal size of a subset of $\{0,1,2\}^n$ which contains no three
    25‚Üípoints on a line. Is it true that $f_3(n) = o(3^n)$?
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíOriginally considered by Moser. It is trivial that $f_3(n) \ge R_3(3^n)$, the maximal
    28‚Üísize of a subset of $\{1,\ldots,3^n\}$ without a three-term arithmetic progression.
    29‚ÜíMoser showed that $f_3(n) \gg 3^n / \sqrt{n}$. The answer is yes, which is a corollary of
    30‚Üíthe density Hales-Jewett theorem, proved by Furstenberg and Katznelson [FuKa91].
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[Er73] ErdŇĎs, P., *Problems and results on combinatorial number theory*. A survey of
    33‚Üícombinatorial theory (Proc. Internat. Sympos., Colorado State Univ., Fort Collins, Colo.,
    34‚Üí1971) (1973), 117-138.
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí[FuKa91] Furstenberg, H. and Katznelson, Y., *A density version of the Hales-Jewett
    37‚Üítheorem*. J. Anal. Math. 57 (1991), 64-119.
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üínamespace Erdos185
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- A combinatorial line in $\{0,1,2\}^n$ consists of three points parameterized by
    43‚Üí$j \in \{0,1,2\}$, such that there exists a nonempty set $S$ of "active" coordinates
    44‚Üíwhere the $j$-th point has value $j$ at each active coordinate, and all three points
    45‚Üíagree at non-active coordinates. -/
    46‚Üídef IsCombinatorialLine (n : ‚Ąē) (p : Fin 3 ‚Üí (Fin n ‚Üí Fin 3)) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  ‚ąÉ (S : Finset (Fin n)), S.Nonempty ‚ąß
    48‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (c : Fin n ‚Üí Fin 3),
    49‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (j : Fin 3) (i : Fin n),
    50‚Üí        p j i = if i ‚ąą S then j else c i
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí/--
    53‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 185 (Density Hales-Jewett for $k=3$) [Er73]:
    54‚Üí
    55‚ÜíFor every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $N$ such that for all $n \ge N$, every subset
    56‚Üí$A \subseteq \{0,1,2\}^n$ of size $|A| > \varepsilon \cdot 3^n$ contains a combinatorial
    57‚Üíline (three points on a line).
    58‚Üí
    59‚ÜíEquivalently, the maximum size $f_3(n)$ of a line-free subset of $\{0,1,2\}^n$ satisfies
    60‚Üí$f_3(n) = o(3^n)$.
    61‚Üí-/
    62‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    63‚Üítheorem erdos_185 :
    64‚Üí    answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    65‚Üí      ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĶ ‚Üí
    66‚Üí        ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
    67‚Üí          ‚ąÄ A : Finset (Fin n ‚Üí Fin 3),
    68‚Üí            (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) > őĶ * (3 : ‚ĄĚ) ^ n ‚Üí
    69‚Üí            ‚ąÉ p : Fin 3 ‚Üí (Fin n ‚Üí Fin 3),
    70‚Üí              IsCombinatorialLine n p ‚ąß ‚ąÄ j : Fin 3, p j ‚ąą A := by
    71‚Üí  sorry
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üíend Erdos185

    16‚Üíimport FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Order.Filter.Cofinite

    25‚Üínamespace Set
    26‚Üívariable {M : Type*} [CommMonoid M] {A : Set M} {m n : ‚Ąē}
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is a multiplicative basis of order `n` if any element `a : M`
    29‚Üícan be expressed as a product of `n` elements lying in `A`. -/
    30‚Üí@[to_additive
    31‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an additive basis of order `n` if for any element
    32‚Üí`a : M`, it can be expressed as a sum of `n` elements lying in `A`. -/]
    33‚Üídef IsMulBasisOfOrder (A : Set M) (n : ‚Ąē) : Prop := ‚ąÄ a, a ‚ąą A ^ n
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/-- A multiplicative basis of some order. -/
    36‚Üí@[to_additive /-- An additive basis of some order. -/]
    37‚Üídef IsMulBasis (A : Set M) : Prop := ‚ąÉ n, A.IsMulBasisOfOrder n
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí@[to_additive]
    40‚Üílemma IsMulBasisOfOrder.isMulBasis (hA : A.IsMulBasisOfOrder n) : A.IsMulBasis := ‚ü®n, hA‚ü©
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí@[to_additive]
    43‚Üílemma isMulBasisOfOrder_iff :
    44‚Üí    A.IsMulBasisOfOrder n ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ a, ‚ąÉ (f : Fin n ‚Üí M) (_ : ‚ąÄ i, f i ‚ąą A), ‚ąŹ i, f i = a := by
    45‚Üí  have := Set.mem_finset_prod (t := .univ) (f := fun _ : Fin n ‚Ü¶ A)
    46‚Üí  simp_all [IsMulBasisOfOrder]
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- No set is a multiplicative basis of order `0`. -/
    49‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp) /-- No set is an additive basis of order `0`. -/]
    50‚Üílemma not_isMulBasisOfOrder_zero [Nontrivial M] : ¬¨A.IsMulBasisOfOrder 0 := by
    51‚Üí  simp [isMulBasisOfOrder_iff, eq_comm, exists_ne]
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    54‚Üílemma isMulBasisOfOrder_one_iff : A.IsMulBasisOfOrder 1 ‚ÜĒ A = univ := by
    55‚Üí  simp [IsMulBasisOfOrder, eq_univ_iff_forall]
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is a multiplicative basis of order `2` if every `a : M` belongs to `A * A`. -/
    58‚Üí@[to_additive
    59‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an additive basis of order `2` if every `a : M` belongs to `A + A`. -/]
    60‚Üílemma isMulBasisOfOrder_two_iff : A.IsMulBasisOfOrder 2 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ a, a ‚ąą A * A := by
    61‚Üí  simp [IsMulBasisOfOrder, pow_two]
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `n` if the elements that can
    64‚Üíbe expressed as a product of `n` elements lying in `A` is cofinite. -/
    65‚Üí@[to_additive
    66‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `n` if the elements that
    67‚Üícan be expressed as a sum of `n` elements lying in `A` is cofinite. -/]
    68‚Üídef IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder (A : Set M) (n : ‚Ąē) : Prop := ‚ąÄŠ∂† a in cofinite, a ‚ąą A ^ n
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/-- An asymptotic multiplicative basis of some order. -/
    71‚Üí@[to_additive /-- An asymptotic additive basis of some order. -/]
    72‚Üídef IsAsymptoticMulBasis (A : Set M) : Prop := ‚ąÉ n, A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí@[to_additive]
    75‚Üílemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.isAsymptoticMulBasis (hA : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n) :
    76‚Üí    A.IsAsymptoticMulBasis := ‚ü®n, hA‚ü©
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí@[to_additive]
    79‚Üílemma IsMulBasisOfOrder.isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder (hA : IsMulBasisOfOrder A n) :
    80‚Üí    A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n := .of_forall hA
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí@[to_additive]
    83‚Üílemma IsMulBasis.isAsymptoticMulBasis (hA : IsMulBasis A) : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasis := by
    84‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®n, hn‚ü© := hA; exact ‚ü®n, hn.isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder‚ü©
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `n` if a cofinite set of
    87‚Üí`a : M` can be written as `a = a‚āĀ * ... * a‚āô`, where each `aŠĶĘ ‚ąą A`. -/
    88‚Üí@[to_additive
    89‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `n` if a cofinite set of
    90‚Üí`a : M` can be written as `a = a‚āĀ + ... + a‚āô`, where each `aŠĶĘ ‚ąą A`. -/]
    91‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_iff_prod :
    92‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄŠ∂† a in cofinite, ‚ąÉ (f : Fin n ‚Üí M) (_ : ‚ąÄ i, f i ‚ąą A),
    93‚Üí      ‚ąŹ i, f i = a := by
    94‚Üí  have := Set.mem_finset_prod (t := .univ) (f := fun _ : Fin n ‚Ü¶ A)
    95‚Üí  simp_all [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder]
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `2` if a cofinite set of
    98‚Üí`a : M` belongs to `A * A`. -/
    99‚Üí@[to_additive
   100‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `2` if a cofinite set of
   101‚Üí`a : M` belongs to `A + A`. -/]
   102‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_two_iff :
   103‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 2 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄŠ∂† a in cofinite, a ‚ąą A * A := by
   104‚Üí  simp [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, pow_two]
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
   107‚Üíprotected lemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.of_finite [Finite M] :
   108‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n := by simp [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder]
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí/-- If `M` is infinite, then no set `A` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `0`. -/
   111‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)
   112‚Üí/-- If `M` is infinite, then no set `A` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `0`. -/]
   113‚Üílemma not_isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_zero [Infinite M] : ¬¨IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 0 := by
   114‚Üí  simpa [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, ‚Üź not_infinite] using Set.infinite_of_finite_compl (by simp)
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí@[to_additive]
   117‚Üíprotected lemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.ne_zero [Infinite M]
   118‚Üí    (hA : IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n) : n ‚Č† 0 := by rintro rfl; simp at hA
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí@[to_additive]
   121‚Üíprotected lemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.nonempty [Infinite M]
   122‚Üí    (hA : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n) : A.Nonempty := by
   123‚Üí  by_contra!
   124‚Üí  simp [this, IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, hA.ne_zero, finite_univ_iff,
   125‚Üí    _root_.Infinite.not_finite] at hA
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí/-- `A : Set M` is an asymptotic basis of order one iff it is cofinite. -/
   128‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)
   129‚Üí/--`A : Set M` is an asymptotic basis of order one iff it is cofinite. -/]
   130‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_one_iff : IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 1 ‚ÜĒ AŠ∂ú.Finite := by

   135‚Üí@[to_additive]
   136‚Üílemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.mono [CanonicallyOrderedMul M] [IsCancelMul M] (hmn : m ‚Č§ n)
   137‚Üí    (hA : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder m) : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n := by
   138‚Üí  cases finite_or_infinite M
   139‚Üí  ¬∑ simp
   140‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®a, ha‚ü© := hA.nonempty
   141‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®n, rfl‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_le hmn
   142‚Üí  refine ((hA.smul_set (a := a ^ n)).union <| finite_Iio <| a ^ n).subset ?_
   143‚Üí  have : a ^ n ‚ÄĘ (A ^ m)Š∂ú = (a ^ n ‚ÄĘ A ^ m)Š∂ú \ Iio (a ^ n) := by
   144‚Üí    ext x
   145‚Üí    constructor
   146‚Üí    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®y, hy, rfl‚ü©
   147‚Üí      simpa
   148‚Üí    ¬∑ simp only [mem_diff, mem_compl_iff, mem_smul_set, smul_eq_mul, not_exists, not_and, mem_Iio,
   149‚Üí        not_lt, le_iff_exists_mul, and_imp, forall_exists_index]

   152‚Üí  simp only [m.add_comm, pow_add, setOf_mem_eq, this, diff_union_self, subset_def, mem_compl_iff,

   154‚Üí  rintro b hb

   157‚Üí
   158‚Üí/-- For `M` equipped with a directed order, a set is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `1`

   161‚Üí/-- For `M` equipped with a directed order, a set is an asymptotic additive basis of order `1`

   164‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 1 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ a, .Ioi a ‚äÜ A := by
   165‚Üí  simp [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, cofinite_hasBasis_Ioi.eventually_iff, Set.subset_def]
   166‚Üí
   167‚Üívariable [SuccOrder M] [NoMaxOrder M]
   168‚Üí
   169‚Üí/-- For `M` equipped with a directed order, a set is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `1`
   170‚Üíif it contains an infinite tail of elements. -/
   171‚Üí@[to_additive
   172‚Üí/-- For `M` equipped with a directed order, a set is an asymptotic additive basis of order `1`
   173‚Üíif it contains an infinite tail of consecutive naturals. -/]
   174‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_one_iff_Ici :
   175‚Üí      IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 1 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ a, .Ici a ‚äÜ A := by

   177‚Üí

    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí/--
    30‚ÜíGiven a set `S` and an element `b` in an order `ő≤`, where all intervals bounded above are finite,
    31‚Üíwe define the partial density of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the proportion of elements in
    32‚Üí`{x ‚ąą A | x < b}` that lie in `S ‚ą© A`.
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíThis definition was inspired from https://github.com/b-mehta/unit-fractions
    35‚Üí-/
    36‚Üí@[inline]
    37‚Üínoncomputable abbrev partialDensity {ő≤ : Type*} [Preorder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot ő≤]
    38‚Üí    (S : Set ő≤) (A : Set ő≤ := Set.univ) (b : ő≤) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    39‚Üí  (Set.interIio (S ‚ą© A) b).ncard / (Set.interIio A b).ncard
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üítheorem partialDensity_le_one {ő≤ : Type*} [Preorder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot ő≤]
    42‚Üí    (S : Set ő≤) (A : Set ő≤ := Set.univ) (b : ő≤) : S.partialDensity A b ‚Č§ 1 := by
    43‚Üí  apply div_le_one_of_le‚āÄ _ (Nat.cast_nonneg _)
    44‚Üí  exact mod_cast Set.ncard_le_ncard <| Set.inter_subset_inter_left _ inter_subset_right
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/--
    47‚ÜíGiven a set `S` in an order `ő≤`, where all intervals bounded above are finite, we define the upper
    48‚Üídensity of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the limsup of the partial densities of `S`
    49‚Üí(relative to `A`) for `b ‚Üí ‚ąě`.
    50‚Üí-/
    51‚Üínoncomputable def upperDensity {ő≤ : Type*} [Preorder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot ő≤]
    52‚Üí    (S : Set ő≤) (A : Set ő≤ := Set.univ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    53‚Üí  atTop.limsup fun (b : ő≤) ‚Ü¶ S.partialDensity A b
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚ÜíGiven a set `S` in an order `ő≤`, where all intervals bounded above are finite, we define the lower
    57‚Üídensity of `S` (relative to a set `A`) to be the liminf of the partial densities of `S`
    58‚Üí(relative to `A`) for `b ‚Üí ‚ąě`.
    59‚Üí-/
    60‚Üínoncomputable def lowerDensity {ő≤ : Type*} [Preorder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot ő≤]
    61‚Üí    (S : Set ő≤) (A : Set ő≤ := Set.univ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    62‚Üí  atTop.liminf fun (b : ő≤) ‚Ü¶ S.partialDensity A b
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üítheorem lowerDensity_le_one {ő≤ : Type*} [Preorder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot ő≤]
    65‚Üí    (S : Set ő≤) (A : Set ő≤ := Set.univ) : S.lowerDensity A ‚Č§ 1 := by
    66‚Üí  by_cases h : atTop (őĪ := ő≤) = ‚ä•
    67‚Üí  ¬∑ simp [h, Set.lowerDensity, Filter.liminf_eq]
    68‚Üí  ¬∑ have : (atTop (őĪ := ő≤)).NeBot := ‚ü®h‚ü©
    69‚Üí    apply Real.sSup_le (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?_) one_pos.le
    70‚Üí    simpa using hx.mono fun y hy ‚Ü¶ hy.trans (Set.partialDensity_le_one _ _ y)
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üítheorem lowerDensity_nonneg {ő≤ : Type*} [Preorder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot ő≤]
    73‚Üí    (S : Set ő≤) (A : Set ő≤ := Set.univ) : 0 ‚Č§ S.lowerDensity A := by
    74‚Üí  rw [Set.lowerDensity, Filter.liminf_eq]
    75‚Üí  exact (em _).elim (le_csSup ¬∑ <| .of_forall fun _ ‚Ü¶ by positivity)
    76‚Üí    (Real.sSup_of_not_bddAbove ¬∑ |>.ge)
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí/--
    79‚ÜíA set `S` in an order `ő≤` where all intervals bounded above are finite is said to have
    80‚Üídensity `őĪ : ‚ĄĚ` (relative to a set `A`) if the proportion of `x ‚ąą S` such that `x < n`
    81‚Üíin `A` tends to `őĪ` as `n ‚Üí ‚ąě`.
    82‚Üí
    83‚ÜíWhen `ő≤ = ‚Ąē` this by default defines the natural density of a set
    84‚Üí(i.e., relative to all of `‚Ąē`).
    85‚Üí-/
    86‚Üídef HasDensity {ő≤ : Type*} [Preorder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot ő≤]
    87‚Üí    (S : Set ő≤) (őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) (A : Set ő≤ := Set.univ) : Prop :=
    88‚Üí  Tendsto (fun (b : ő≤) => S.partialDensity A b) atTop (ūĚďĚ őĪ)
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí/--
    91‚ÜíA set `S` in an order `ő≤` where all intervals bounded above are finite is said to have
    92‚Üípositive density (relative to a set `A`) if there exists a positive `őĪ : ‚ĄĚ` such that
    93‚Üí`S` has density `őĪ` (relative to a set `A`).
    94‚Üí-/
    95‚Üídef HasPosDensity {ő≤ : Type*} [Preorder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot ő≤]
    96‚Üí    (S : Set ő≤) (A : Set ő≤ := Set.univ) : Prop :=
    97‚Üí  ‚ąÉ őĪ > 0, S.HasDensity őĪ A

    99‚Üínamespace HasDensity

   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí/-- In a non-trivial partial order with a least element, the set of all
   104‚Üíelements has density one. -/
   105‚Üí@[simp]
   106‚Üítheorem univ {ő≤ : Type*} [PartialOrder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrder ő≤] [OrderBot ő≤] [Nontrivial ő≤] :
   107‚Üí    (@Set.univ ő≤).HasDensity 1 := by
   108‚Üí  by_cases h : atTop (őĪ := ő≤) = ‚ä•
   109‚Üí  ¬∑ simp [h, HasDensity]
   110‚Üí  ¬∑ simp [HasDensity, partialDensity]
   111‚Üí    let ‚ü®b, hb‚ü© := Set.Iio_eventually_ncard_ne_zero ő≤
   112‚Üí    refine tendsto_const_nhds.congr' ?_
   113‚Üí    exact (eventually_ge_atTop b).mono fun n hn ‚Ü¶ (div_self <| mod_cast hb n hn).symm
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üítheorem univ_nat_hasDensity_one : (@Set.univ ‚Ąē).HasDensity 1 := univ
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí@[simp]
   118‚Üítheorem empty {ő≤ : Type*} [Preorder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot ő≤] (A : Set ő≤ := Set.univ) :
   119‚Üí    Set.HasDensity (‚ąÖ : Set ő≤) 0 A := by
   120‚Üí  simpa [HasDensity, partialDensity] using tendsto_const_nhds
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üítheorem mono {ő≤ : Type*} [PartialOrder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrder ő≤] [OrderBot ő≤]
   123‚Üí    {S T : Set ő≤} {őĪS őĪT : ‚ĄĚ} [(atTop (őĪ := ő≤)).NeBot] (h : S ‚äÜ T) (hS : S.HasDensity őĪS)
   124‚Üí    (hT : T.HasDensity őĪT) : őĪS ‚Č§ őĪT := by
   125‚Üí  rw [HasDensity] at hS hT
   126‚Üí  apply le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto hS hT
   127‚Üí  filter_upwards [eventually_ge_atTop ‚ä•] with b hb
   128‚Üí  apply div_le_div_of_nonneg_right
   129‚Üí  grw [Set.ncard_le_ncard (inter_subset_inter_left _ (inter_subset_inter_left _ h))]
   130‚Üí  exact Nat.cast_nonneg _
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üítheorem nonneg {ő≤ : Type*} [Preorder ő≤] [LocallyFiniteOrderBot ő≤] [(atTop : Filter ő≤).NeBot]
   133‚Üí    {S : Set ő≤} {őĪ : ‚ĄĚ} (h : S.HasDensity őĪ) : 0 ‚Č§ őĪ :=
   134‚Üí  le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto' empty h fun b => by simp [div_nonneg, partialDensity]
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üíend Set.HasDensity

   142‚Üí/--
   143‚ÜíThe natural density of the set of even numbers is `1 / 2`.
   144‚Üí-/
   145‚Üítheorem hasDensity_even : {n : ‚Ąē | Even n}.HasDensity (1 / 2) := by
   146‚Üí  simp [HasDensity, partialDensity, Set.interIio]
   147‚Üí  have h {n : ‚Ąē} (hn : 1 ‚Č§ n) : (({n : ‚Ąē | Even n} ‚ą© Iio n).ncard : ‚ĄĚ) / n =
   148‚Üí      if Even n then 2‚ĀĽ¬Ļ else (n + 1 : ‚ĄĚ) /  n * 2‚ĀĽ¬Ļ := by
   149‚Üí    split_ifs with h
   150‚Üí    ¬∑ rw [‚Üź image_mul_two_Iio_even h, ncard_image_of_injective _
   151‚Üí          (mul_right_injective‚āÄ (by simp)), ncard_Iio,
   152‚Üí        cast_div_charZero (even_iff_two_dvd.mp h), cast_ofNat,
   153‚Üí        div_div_cancel_left' <| cast_ne_zero.2 (by linarith)]
   154‚Üí    ¬∑ replace h : Even (n + 1) := by simpa [Nat.even_add', ‚Üź Nat.not_even_iff_odd]
   155‚Üí      rw [‚Üź image_mul_two_Iio n, ncard_image_of_injective _
   156‚Üí          (mul_right_injective‚āÄ (by simp)), ncard_Iio,
   157‚Üí        cast_div (even_iff_two_dvd.mp h) (by norm_num), cast_add]; ring
   158‚Üí  refine Tendsto.congr' (eventually_atTop.2 ‚ü®1, fun n hn => (h hn).symm‚ü©)
   159‚Üí    (Tendsto.if' tendsto_const_nhds ?_)
   160‚Üí  replace h : Tendsto (fun (k : ‚Ąē) => 1 + 1 / (k : ‚ĄĚ)) atTop (ūĚďĚ 1) := by
   161‚Üí    simpa using Tendsto.const_add (M := ‚ĄĚ) _ tendsto_one_div_atTop_nhds_zero_nat
   162‚Üí  simpa using Tendsto.mul_const _ <|
   163‚Üí    Tendsto.congr' (eventually_atTop.2 ‚ü®1, fun k hk => by field_simp‚ü©) h
   164‚Üí
   165‚Üí/-- A finite set has natural density zero. -/
   166‚Üítheorem hasDensity_zero_of_finite {S : Set ‚Ąē} (h : S.Finite) : S.HasDensity 0 := by
   167‚Üí  simp [HasDensity, partialDensity, Set.interIio]
   168‚Üí  have (n : ‚Ąē) : ((S ‚ą© Set.Iio n).ncard : ‚ĄĚ) / n ‚Č§ S.ncard / n := by
   169‚Üí    by_cases h‚āÄ : n = 0; simp [h‚āÄ]
   170‚Üí    exact div_le_div‚āÄ (by simp) (by simpa using Set.ncard_inter_le_ncard_left _ _ h)
   171‚Üí      (by simpa using n.pos_of_ne_zero h‚āÄ) le_rfl
   172‚Üí  exact tendsto_of_tendsto_of_tendsto_of_le_of_le tendsto_const_nhds
   173‚Üí    (tendsto_const_div_atTop_nhds_zero_nat _)
   174‚Üí    (fun _ => div_nonneg (cast_nonneg _) (cast_nonneg _)) this
   175‚Üí
   176‚Üí/-- A set of positive natural density is infinite. -/
   177‚Üítheorem infinite_of_hasDensity_pos {S : Set ‚Ąē} {őĪ : ‚ĄĚ} (h : S.HasDensity őĪ) (hőĪ : őĪ ‚Č† 0) :
   178‚Üí    S.Infinite :=
   179‚Üí  mt hasDensity_zero_of_finite fun h' => hőĪ (tendsto_nhds_unique h h')

   183‚Üí/-! ## Logarithmic Density -/
   184‚Üí
   185‚Üísection LogarithmicDensity
   186‚Üí
   187‚Üíopen Finset Real Classical
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí/--
   190‚ÜíA set `A` of natural numbers has logarithmic density `d` if the sequence
   191‚Üí$(1 / \log n) \cdot \sum_{k \in A, k \le n} (1/k)$ converges to `d`.
   192‚ÜíLogarithmic density is a weaker notion than natural density: if a set has natural density `d`,
   193‚Üíthen it also has logarithmic density `d` (see `Set.HasDensity.hasLogDensity`), but the converse
   194‚Üíis false.
   195‚Üí-/
   196‚Üídef Set.HasLogDensity (A : Set ‚Ąē) (d : ‚ĄĚ) : Prop :=
   197‚Üí  Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ąē => (‚ąĎ k ‚Č§ n with k ‚ąą A, (k : ‚ĄĚ)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ / .log n : ‚ĄĚ)) atTop (ūĚďĚ d)
   198‚Üí
   199‚Üí/-- If a set has natural density `d`, then it also has logarithmic density `d`. -/
   200‚Üíproof_wanted Set.HasDensity.hasLogDensity {A : Set ‚Ąē} {d : ‚ĄĚ} (h : A.HasDensity d) : A.HasLogDensity d
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üíend LogarithmicDensity

    26‚Üí/--
    27‚ÜíA set $A$ is said to be sum-free if the sumset $A + A$ is disjoint from $A$, i.e.
    28‚Üíif the equation $a + b = c$ has no solution with $a, b, c \in A$.

    30‚Üídef IsSumFree (A : Set őĪ) : Prop := Disjoint (A + A) A

    32‚Üí/-- A set $A$ of natural numbers is said to have bounded gaps if there exists an integer $p$ such

    36‚Üí/-- A Sidon set is a set, such that such that all pairwise sums of elements are distinct apart from
    37‚Üícoincidences forced by the commutativity of addition. -/
    38‚Üídef IsSidon (A : Set őĪ) : Prop := ‚ąÄŠĶČ (i‚āĀ ‚ąą A) (j‚āĀ ‚ąą A) (i‚āā ‚ąą A) (j‚āā ‚ąą A),
    39‚Üí  i‚āĀ + i‚āā = j‚āĀ + j‚āā ‚Üí (i‚āĀ = j‚āĀ ‚ąß i‚āā = j‚āā) ‚ą® (i‚āĀ = j‚āā ‚ąß i‚āā = j‚āĀ)

    43‚Üílemma IsSidon.avoids_isAPOfLength_three {A : Set ‚Ąē} (hA : IsSidon A)
    44‚Üí    {Y : Set ‚Ąē} (hY : Y.IsAPOfLength 3) :
    45‚Üí    (A ‚ą© Y).ncard ‚Č§ 2 := by
    46‚Üí  simp [IsAPOfLength, IsAPOfLengthWith] at hY
    47‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®hc, ‚ü®a, d, hY‚ü©‚ü© := hY

    49‚Üí  by_contra! h
    50‚Üí  have hss : Y ‚äÜ A ‚ą© Y := by
    51‚Üí    have hY_fin : Finite Y := finite_of_ncard_ne_zero (by linarith)
    52‚Üí    rw [Set.eq_of_subset_of_ncard_le (Set.inter_subset_right) (by linarith)]
    53‚Üí  have ha : a ‚ąą A := mem_of_mem_inter_left <| hss (hY ‚Ėł ‚ü®0, by norm_num, by simp‚ü©)
    54‚Üí  have ha‚āĀ : a + d ‚ąą A := mem_of_mem_inter_left <| hss (hY ‚Ėł ‚ü®1, by norm_num, by simp‚ü©)
    55‚Üí  have ha‚āā : a + 2 ‚ÄĘ d ‚ąą A := mem_of_mem_inter_left <| hss (hY ‚Ėł ‚ü®2, by norm_num, by simp‚ü©)
    56‚Üí  have := hA _ ha _ ha‚āĀ _ ha‚āā _ ha‚āĀ (by simp; omega)
    57‚Üí  simp at this
    58‚Üí  simp [hY, this.1, setOf_and] at hY_card
    59‚Üí  linarith [ncard_singleton _ ‚Ėł ncard_inter_le_ncard_right {a | ‚ąÉ x, x < 3} {a}]
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üítheorem IsSidon.subset {A B : Set őĪ} (hB : IsSidon B) (hAB : A ‚äÜ B) : IsSidon A :=
    62‚Üí  fun _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ‚Ü¶ hB _ (hAB ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ) _ (hAB ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ) _ (hAB ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ) _ (hAB ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ) ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üítheorem IsSidon.insert {A : Set őĪ} {m : őĪ} [IsRightCancelAdd őĪ] [IsLeftCancelAdd őĪ]
    65‚Üí    (hA : IsSidon A) :
    66‚Üí    IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {m}) ‚ÜĒ (m ‚ąą A ‚ą® ‚ąÄŠĶČ (a ‚ąą A) (b ‚ąą A), m + m ‚Č† a + b ‚ąß ‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, m + a ‚Č† b + c) := by
    67‚Üí  by_cases h_mem : m ‚ąą A
    68‚Üí  ¬∑ exact ‚ü®fun _ ‚Ü¶ .inl h_mem, fun _ ‚Ü¶ by rwa [union_singleton, insert_eq_of_mem h_mem]‚ü©
    69‚Üí  refine ‚ü®fun h ‚Ü¶ .inr fun a ha b hb ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®fun hc ‚Ü¶ ?_, fun c hc h_contr ‚Ü¶ ?_‚ü©, fun hm ‚Ü¶ ?_‚ü©
    70‚Üí  ¬∑ exact h m (by simp) a (by simp [ha]) m (by simp) b (by simp [hb]) hc
    71‚Üí      |>.elim (fun _ ‚Ü¶ by simp_all) (fun _ ‚Ü¶ by simp_all)
    72‚Üí  ¬∑ exact h m (by simp) b (by simp [hb]) a (by simp [ha]) c (by simp [hc]) h_contr
    73‚Üí      |>.elim (fun _ ‚Ü¶ by simp_all) (fun _ ‚Ü¶ by simp_all)
    74‚Üí  ¬∑ intro i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ
    75‚Üí    rcases hi‚āĀ with (hi‚āĀ | hi‚āĀ)
    76‚Üí    ¬∑ intro j‚āĀ hj‚āĀ
    77‚Üí      rcases hj‚āĀ with (hj‚āĀ | hj‚āĀ)
    78‚Üí      ¬∑ intro i‚āā hi‚āā
    79‚Üí        rcases hi‚āā with (hi‚āā | hi‚āā)
    80‚Üí        ¬∑ intro j‚āā hj‚āā
    81‚Üí          rcases hj‚āā with (hj‚āā | hj‚āā)
    82‚Üí          ¬∑ exact fun h ‚Ü¶ hA i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ j‚āĀ hj‚āĀ i‚āā hi‚āā j‚āā hj‚āā h
    83‚Üí          ¬∑ simp_all
    84‚Üí            exact fun h ‚Ü¶ by cases (hm j‚āĀ hj‚āĀ i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ).2 i‚āā hi‚āā (add_comm j‚āĀ m ‚Ėł h.symm)
    85‚Üí        ¬∑ simp_all
    86‚Üí          exact fun a ha h ‚Ü¶ by cases (hm i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ j‚āĀ hj‚āĀ).2 a ha (add_comm i‚āĀ m ‚Ėł h)
    87‚Üí      ¬∑ simp_all
    88‚Üí        refine ‚ü®fun b hb h ‚Ü¶ .inr <| by simp_all [add_comm], fun b hb ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®fun h ‚Ü¶ ?_, ?_‚ü©‚ü©
    89‚Üí        ¬∑ cases (hm i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ b hb).1 h.symm
    90‚Üí        ¬∑ exact fun c hc h ‚Ü¶ by cases ((hm c hc i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ).2 b hb) h.symm
    91‚Üí    ¬∑ simp_all
    92‚Üí      exact fun _ _ _ _ _ ‚Ü¶ by simp_all [add_comm]
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí
    95‚Üí/-!
    96‚ÜíMaximal Sidon sets in an interval.
    97‚Üí
    98‚ÜíWe follow the convention that `IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N` means `A ‚äÜ {1, ‚Ä¶, N}` is Sidon and
    99‚Üíis inclusion-maximal among subsets of `Set.Icc 1 N` with the Sidon property.
   100‚Üí-/
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí/-- `IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N` means `A ‚äÜ {1, ‚Ä¶, N}` is Sidon and cannot be extended within
   103‚Üí`{1, ‚Ä¶, N}` while remaining Sidon. -/
   104‚Üídef IsMaximalSidonSetIn (A : Set ‚Ąē) (N : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
   105‚Üí  A ‚äÜ Set.Icc 1 N ‚ąß IsSidon A ‚ąß
   106‚Üí    ‚ąÄ ‚¶Éx : ‚Ąē‚¶Ą, x ‚ąą Set.Icc 1 N ‚Üí x ‚ąČ A ‚Üí ¬¨ IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {x})
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üínamespace IsMaximalSidonSetIn
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, ‚Ä¶, N}`, then `A ‚äÜ {1, ‚Ä¶, N}`. -/
   111‚Üítheorem subset {A : Set ‚Ąē} {N : ‚Ąē} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) :
   112‚Üí    A ‚äÜ Set.Icc 1 N := hA.1
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, ‚Ä¶, N}`, then `A` is Sidon. -/
   115‚Üítheorem isSidon {A : Set ‚Ąē} {N : ‚Ąē} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) : IsSidon A := hA.2.1
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí/-- Maximality condition unpacked. -/
   118‚Üítheorem maximal {A : Set ‚Ąē} {N : ‚Ąē} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) {x : ‚Ąē}
   119‚Üí    (hx : x ‚ąą Set.Icc 1 N) (hxA : x ‚ąČ A) : ¬¨ IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {x}) := hA.2.2 hx hxA
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üíend IsMaximalSidonSetIn
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üíend Set
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üínamespace Finset
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üíinstance (A : Finset őĪ) [DecidableEq őĪ] : Decidable (IsSidon (A : Set őĪ)) :=
   128‚Üí  decidable_of_iff (‚ąÄŠĶČ (i‚āĀ ‚ąą A) (j‚āĀ ‚ąą A) (i‚āā ‚ąą A) (j‚āā ‚ąą A), _) <| by rfl
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí/-- The maximum size of a Sidon set in the supplied `Finset`. -/
   131‚Üídef maxSidonSubsetCard (A : Finset őĪ) [DecidableEq őĪ] : ‚Ąē :=
   132‚Üí  (A.powerset.filter fun B : Finset őĪ ‚Ü¶ IsSidon (B : Set őĪ)).sup Finset.card
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí/-- If `A` is finite Sidon, then `A ‚ą™ {s}` is also Sidon provided `s ‚Č• A.max + 1`. -/
   135‚Üítheorem IsSidon.insert_ge_max' {A : Finset ‚Ąē} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ‚Ąē)) {s : ‚Ąē}
   136‚Üí    (hs : 2 * A.max' h + 1 ‚Č§ s) :
   137‚Üí    IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {s}) := by
   138‚Üí  have h‚āĀ {a b c : ‚Ąē} (ha : a ‚ąą A) (hb : b ‚ąą A) (hc : c ‚ąą A) :
   139‚Üí        a + b < 2 * A.max' h + 1 + c := by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb]
   140‚Üí  have : s ‚ąČ A := by
   141‚Üí    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ |>.2 fun a ha ‚Ü¶ by
   142‚Üí      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   143‚Üí  exact (IsSidon.insert hA).2 <| by simpa [this] using fun a ha b hb ‚Ü¶
   144‚Üí    ‚ü®by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb], fun c hc ‚Ü¶ by linarith [h‚āĀ hc hb ha]‚ü©
   145‚Üí
   146‚Üítheorem IsSidon.exists_insert {A : Finset ‚Ąē} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ‚Ąē)) :
   147‚Üí    ‚ąÉ m ‚ąČ A, IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {m}) := by
   148‚Üí  refine ‚ü®2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, insert_ge_max' h hA le_rfl‚ü©
   149‚Üí  exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ |>.2 fun a ha ‚Ü¶ by
   150‚Üí    linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üítheorem IsSidon.exists_insert_ge {A : Finset ‚Ąē} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ‚Ąē)) (s : ‚Ąē) :
   153‚Üí    ‚ąÉ m ‚Č• s, m ‚ąČ A ‚ąß IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {m}) := by
   154‚Üí  refine ‚ü®if s ‚Č• 2 * A.max' h + 1 then s else 2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©
   155‚Üí  ¬∑ split_ifs <;> linarith
   156‚Üí  ¬∑ split_ifs <;>
   157‚Üí    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ |>.2 fun a ha ‚Ü¶ by
   158‚Üí      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   159‚Üí  ¬∑ split_ifs with hs
   160‚Üí    ¬∑ exact insert_ge_max' h hA hs
   161‚Üí    ¬∑ exact insert_ge_max' h hA le_rfl
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üíend Finset
   164‚Üí

    16‚Üíimport FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Algebra.Group.Indicator
    17‚Üíimport Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.Basic
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Convolution of Functions on ‚Ąē
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíThis file defines the sum (`‚ąó`) convolution of functions `‚Ąē ‚Üí R`.
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí## Main Definitions
    25‚Üí* `AdditiveCombinatorics.sumConv`: The sum convolution `f ‚ąó g`.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí## Notation
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí* `f ‚ąó g` for `sumConv f g`.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí## TODO
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí* `f ‚ąė g` for `diffConv f g`.
    35‚Üí-/
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace AdditiveCombinatorics
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üíopen Finset Classical Set
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üívariable {R : Type*} [Semiring R]
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- The sum convolution of two functions `f, g : ‚Ąē ‚Üí R`, also known as the Cauchy product.
    45‚Üí`(f ‚ąó g) n = ‚ąĎ_{a+b=n} f(a)g(b)`. -/
    46‚Üídef sumConv (f g : ‚Ąē ‚Üí R) (n : ‚Ąē) : R := ‚ąĎ p ‚ąą antidiagonal n, f p.1 * g p.2
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üíinfixl:70 " ‚ąó " => sumConv
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- The number of sum representations is the sum convolution of `A`'s indicator
    51‚Üífunction with itself: $1_A\ast 1_A(n)$. -/
    52‚Üínoncomputable def sumRep (A : Set ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē := (ūĚüô_A ‚ąó ūĚüô_A)
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí@[simp]
    56‚Üílemma sumRep_def (A : Set ‚Ąē) (n : ‚Ąē) :
    57‚Üí    sumRep A n = ((antidiagonal n).filter (fun (p : ‚Ąē √ó ‚Ąē) ‚Ü¶ p.1 ‚ąą A ‚ąß p.2 ‚ąą A)).card := by
    58‚Üí  simp only [sumRep, sumConv, indicatorOne, indicator, mul_ite, mul_one, mul_zero]
    59‚Üí  push_cast [‚Üź ite_and, card_filter, and_comm]
    60‚Üí  congr!
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üíopen PowerSeries
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üítheorem sumRep_eq_powerSeries_coeff (A : Set ‚Ąē) (n : ‚Ąē) : (sumRep A n : ‚Ąē) =
    65‚Üí    ((PowerSeries.mk (ūĚüô_A)) * (PowerSeries.mk (ūĚüô_A)) : PowerSeries ‚Ąē).coeff n := by
    66‚Üí  simp [sumRep, sumConv, indicatorOne, indicator, PowerSeries.coeff_mul, PowerSeries.coeff_mk]
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üíend AdditiveCombinatorics

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport Mathlib.Algebra.Module.NatInt
    18‚Üíimport Mathlib.Data.ENat.Lattice
    19‚Üíimport Mathlib.Data.Set.Card
    20‚Üíimport Mathlib.Tactic.IntervalCases
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üíopen scoped Classical
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí/-! # Arithmetic Progressions
    25‚Üí
    26‚ÜíMain definitions:
    27‚Üí- `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : Set őĪ) (l : ‚Ąē‚ąě) (a d : őĪ)` : predicate asserting that `s` is the
    28‚Üí  set consisting of an arithmetic progression of length `l` (possibly infinite) with first term
    29‚Üí  `a` and difference `d`. Useful for cases in which additional conditions need to be applied to
    30‚Üí  the individual terms and/or difference.
    31‚Üí- `Set.IsAPOfLength (s : Set őĪ) (l : ‚Ąē‚ąě)` : predicate asserting that `s` is the set consisting
    32‚Üí  of an arithmetic progression of length `l`, for some some first term and difference.
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üívariable {őĪ : Type*} [AddCommMonoid őĪ]
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/--
    38‚ÜíA set $S$ is an arithmetic progression of length $l$ with first term $a$ and difference $d$
    39‚Üíif $S = \{a, a + d, ..., a + (l - 1)d\}$, if $l$ if finite, else $S = \{a, a + d, a + 2d, ...\}.
    40‚ÜíThis can be written as `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith a l a d`, where `l : ‚Ąē‚ąě` may take the infinite
    41‚Üívalue `‚ä§`.
    42‚Üí-/
    43‚Üídef Set.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : Set őĪ) (l : ‚Ąē‚ąě) (a d : őĪ) : Prop :=
    44‚Üí  ENat.card s = l ‚ąß s = {a + n ‚ÄĘ d | (n : ‚Ąē) (_ : n < l)}
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/--
    47‚ÜíA list version of `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith`. Useful when order preservation is required, for example
    48‚Üíwhen considering images under arbitrary functions.
    49‚Üí-/
    50‚Üídef List.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : List őĪ) (l : ‚Ąē) (a d : őĪ) : Prop :=
    51‚Üí  s = (List.range l).map (fun n ‚Ü¶ a + n ‚ÄĘ d) ‚ą® s = (List.range l).reverse.map (fun n ‚Ü¶ a + n ‚ÄĘ d)
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üínamespace Set.IsAPOfLengthWith
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üívariable {s : Set őĪ} {l : ‚Ąē‚ąě} {a d : őĪ}
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üítheorem card (h : s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d) : ENat.card s = l := h.1
    58‚Üítheorem eq (h : s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d) : s = {a + n ‚ÄĘ d | (n : ‚Ąē) (_ : n < l)} := h.2
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length zero if and only
    61‚Üíif `s` is empty. -/
    62‚Üí@[simp]
    63‚Üítheorem zero : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 0 a d ‚ÜĒ s = ‚ąÖ := by simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length one if and only
    66‚Üíif `s` is a singleton. -/
    67‚Üí@[simp]
    68‚Üítheorem one : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 1 a d ‚ÜĒ s = {a} := by simp +contextual [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üíend Set.IsAPOfLengthWith
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üínamespace List.IsAPOfLengthWith
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üívariable {s : List őĪ} {l : ‚Ąē} {a d : őĪ}
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üítheorem length (h : s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d) : s.length = l := by
    77‚Üí  cases h <;> simp_all
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length zero if and only
    80‚Üíif `s` is empty. -/
    81‚Üí@[simp]
    82‚Üítheorem zero : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 0 a d ‚ÜĒ s = [] := by
    83‚Üí  simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length one if and only
    86‚Üíif `s` is a singleton. -/
    87‚Üí@[simp]
    88‚Üítheorem one : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 1 a d ‚ÜĒ s = [a] := by
    89‚Üí  simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üíend List.IsAPOfLengthWith
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí/-- In an abelian additive group `őĪ`, the set `{a, b}` with `a ‚Č† b` is an arithmetic progression of
    94‚Üílength `2` with first term `a` and difference `b - a`. -/
    95‚Üítheorem Set.isAPOfLengthWith_pair {őĪ : Type*} [DecidableEq őĪ] [AddCommGroup őĪ]
    96‚Üí    {a b : őĪ} (hab : a ‚Č† b) :
    97‚Üí    Set.IsAPOfLengthWith {a, b} 2 a (b - a) := by
    98‚Üí  simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    99‚Üí  rw [Finset.card_insert_of_notMem (by simpa only [Finset.mem_singleton])]
   100‚Üí  simp
   101‚Üí  refine Set.ext fun x => ‚ü®fun h ‚Ü¶ ?_, fun ‚ü®n, ‚ü®_, _‚ü©‚ü© ‚Ü¶ by interval_cases n <;> simp_all‚ü©
   102‚Üí  cases h with
   103‚Üí  | inl hl => use 0; simp [hl]
   104‚Üí  | inr hr => exact ‚ü®1, by norm_num, by simp_all‚ü©
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí-- Formalisation note: separate result needed for `‚Ąē` since this is not covered by
   107‚Üí-- the `AddCommGroup` result above.
   108‚Üí/-- The set `{a, b} : Set ‚Ąē` with `a < b` is an arithmetic progression of length `2` with
   109‚Üífirst term `a` and difference `b - a`. -/
   110‚Üítheorem Nat.isAPOfLengthWith_pair {a b : ‚Ąē} (hab : a < b) :
   111‚Üí    Set.IsAPOfLengthWith {a, b} 2 a (b - a) := by
   112‚Üí  let ‚ü®n, h‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_lt hab
   113‚Üí  simp [Set.IsAPOfLengthWith, h, add_assoc]
   114‚Üí  exact Set.ext fun x => ‚ü®fun a => by aesop, fun ‚ü®w, ‚ü®_, _‚ü©‚ü© => by interval_cases w <;> simp_all‚ü©
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí/--
   117‚ÜíThe predicate that a set `s` is an arithmetic progression of length `l` (possibly infinite).
   118‚ÜíThis predicate does not assert a specific value for the first term or the difference of the
   119‚Üíarithmetic progression.
   120‚Üí-/
   121‚Üídef Set.IsAPOfLength (s : Set őĪ) (l : ‚Ąē‚ąě) : Prop :=
   122‚Üí  ‚ąÉ a d : őĪ, s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí/--
   125‚ÜíA list version of `Set.IsAPOfLength`. Useful when order preservation is required, for example
   126‚Üíwhen considering images under arbitrary functions.
   127‚Üí-/
   128‚Üídef List.IsAPOfLength (s : List őĪ) (l : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
   129‚Üí  ‚ąÉ a d : őĪ, s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üínamespace Set.IsAPOfLength
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üíopen Set.IsAPOfLengthWith
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üívariable {s : Set őĪ} {l : ‚Ąē‚ąě}
   136‚Üí
   137‚Üítheorem card (h : s.IsAPOfLength l) : ENat.card s = l := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üítheorem eq (h : s.IsAPOfLength l) : ‚ąÉ a d : őĪ, s = {a + n ‚ÄĘ d | (n : ‚Ąē) (_ : n < l)} :=
   140‚Üí  ‚ü®h.choose, h.choose_spec.choose, h.choose_spec.choose_spec.2‚ü©
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí/-- Only the empty set is a finite arithmetic progression of length $0$. -/
   143‚Üí@[simp] theorem zero : s.IsAPOfLength 0 ‚ÜĒ s = ‚ąÖ := by simp [Set.IsAPOfLength]
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí/-- Only singletons are finite arithmetic progressions of length $1$. -/
   146‚Üí@[simp] theorem one : s.IsAPOfLength 1 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ a, s = {a} := by simp [IsAPOfLength]
   147‚Üí
   148‚Üí/-- If a set is an arithmetic progression of lengths `l‚āĀ` and `l‚āā`, then the lengths are
   149‚Üíequal. -/
   150‚Üítheorem congr {s : Set őĪ} {l‚āĀ l‚āā : ‚Ąē‚ąě}
   151‚Üí    (h‚āĀ : s.IsAPOfLength l‚āĀ) (h‚āā : s.IsAPOfLength l‚āā) :
   152‚Üí    l‚āĀ = l‚āā := by
   153‚Üí  rw [‚Üź h‚āĀ.card, h‚āā.card]
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üíend Set.IsAPOfLength
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üínamespace List.IsAPOfLength
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üíopen List.IsAPOfLengthWith
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üívariable {s : List őĪ} {l : ‚Ąē}
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üítheorem length (h : s.IsAPOfLength l) : s.length = l := by
   164‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®_, _, h‚ü© := h
   165‚Üí  exact h.length
   166‚Üí
   167‚Üí/-- Only the empty list is a finite arithmetic progression of length $0$. -/
   168‚Üí@[simp] theorem zero : s.IsAPOfLength 0 ‚ÜĒ s = [] := by simp [IsAPOfLength]
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí/-- Only singletons are finite arithmetic progressions of length $1$. -/
   171‚Üí@[simp] theorem one : s.IsAPOfLength 1 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ a, s = [a] := by simp [IsAPOfLength]
   172‚Üí
   173‚Üí/-- If a list is an arithmetic progression of lengths `l‚āĀ` and `l‚āā`, then the lengths are
   174‚Üíequal. -/
   175‚Üítheorem congr {s : List őĪ} {l‚āĀ l‚āā : ‚Ąē}
   176‚Üí    (h‚āĀ : s.IsAPOfLength l‚āĀ) (h‚āā : s.IsAPOfLength l‚āā) :
   177‚Üí    l‚āĀ = l‚āā := by
   178‚Üí  rw [‚Üź h‚āĀ.length, h‚āā.length]
   179‚Üí
   180‚Üíend List.IsAPOfLength
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üítheorem Set.isAPOfLength_pair {őĪ : Type*} [DecidableEq őĪ] [AddCommGroup őĪ] {a b : őĪ} (hab : a ‚Č† b) :
   183‚Üí    Set.IsAPOfLength {a, b} 2 :=
   184‚Üí  ‚ü®a, b - a, Set.isAPOfLengthWith_pair hab‚ü©
   185‚Üí
   186‚Üítheorem Nat.isAPOfLength_pair {a b : ‚Ąē} (hab : a < b) :
   187‚Üí    Set.IsAPOfLength {a, b} 2 :=
   188‚Üí  ‚ü®a, b - a, Nat.isAPOfLengthWith_pair hab‚ü©
   189‚Üí
   190‚Üí/-- The empty set is not an arithmetic progression of positive length. -/
   191‚Üítheorem Set.not_isAPOfLength_empty {l : ‚Ąē‚ąě} (hl : 0 < l) :
   192‚Üí    ¬¨Set.IsAPOfLength (‚ąÖ : Set őĪ) l :=
   193‚Üí  fun h ‚Ü¶ by simp_all [h.congr <| Set.IsAPOfLength.zero.2 rfl]
   194‚Üí
   195‚Üí/-- We say that a set `s` is free of arithmetic progressions of length `l` if `s` contains no
   196‚Üínon-trivial arithmetic progressions of length `l`. Written as `Set.IsAPOfLengthFree s l`. --/
   197‚Üídef Set.IsAPOfLengthFree (s : Set őĪ) (l : ‚Ąē‚ąě) : Prop :=
   198‚Üí  ‚ąÄ t ‚äÜ s, t.IsAPOfLength l ‚Üí l ‚Č§ 1
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí/-- Any set is free of arithmetic progressions of length `1`, because such APs are all trivial. -/
   201‚Üítheorem Set.isAPOfLengthFree_one (s : Set őĪ) : s.IsAPOfLengthFree 1 := by
   202‚Üí  simp [Set.IsAPOfLengthFree]
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí/-- Any set is free of arithmetic progressions of length `0`, because such APs are all trivial. -/
   205‚Üítheorem Set.isAPOfLengthFree_zero (s : Set őĪ) : s.IsAPOfLengthFree 0 := by
   206‚Üí  simp [Set.IsAPOfLengthFree]
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí/-- Any non-trivial arithmetic progression cannot be free of arithmetic progressions. -/
   209‚Üítheorem Set.IsAPOfLength.not_isAPOfLengthFree {s : Set őĪ} {l : ‚Ąē‚ąě}
   210‚Üí    (hs : s.IsAPOfLength l) (hl : 1 < l) : ¬¨s.IsAPOfLengthFree l := by
   211‚Üí  simpa [Set.IsAPOfLengthFree] using ‚ü®s, le_rfl, ‚ü®hs, hl‚ü©‚ü©
   212‚Üí
   213‚Üí/--
   214‚ÜíDefine the largest possible size of a subset of $\{1, \dots, N\}$ that does not contain
   215‚Üíany non-trivial $k$-term arithmetic progression.
   216‚Üí-/
   217‚Üínoncomputable def Set.IsAPOfLengthFree.maxCard (k : ‚Ąē) (N : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
   218‚Üí  sSup {Finset.card S | (S) (_ : S ‚äÜ Finset.Icc 1 N) (_ : (S : Set ‚Ąē).IsAPOfLengthFree k)}
   219‚Üí
   220‚Üítheorem Set.IsAPOfLengthFree.maxCard_zero (N : ‚Ąē) : maxCard 0 N = N := by
   221‚Üí  simp only [maxCard, Nat.cast_zero, isAPOfLengthFree_zero, exists_const, exists_prop]
   222‚Üí  apply IsGreatest.csSup_eq
   223‚Üí  refine ‚ü®‚ü®Finset.Icc 1 N, Subset.rfl, Nat.card_Icc _ _‚ü©, ?_‚ü©
   224‚Üí  rintro n ‚ü®S, hS, rfl‚ü©
   225‚Üí  exact S.card_mono hS |>.trans_eq (Nat.card_Icc _ _)
   226‚Üí
   227‚Üítheorem Set.IsAPOfLengthFree.maxCard_one (N : ‚Ąē) : maxCard 1 N = N := by
   228‚Üí  nth_rw 2 [‚Üź maxCard_zero N]
   229‚Üí  simp [maxCard, isAPOfLengthFree_one, isAPOfLengthFree_zero]
   230‚Üí
   231‚Üí/-- A set `A` contains an arithmetic progression of length `k` with difference `d`. -/
   232‚Üídef Set.ContainsAP (A : Set őĪ) (k : ‚Ąē) (d : őĪ) : Prop :=
   233‚Üí  ‚ąÉ a, ‚ąÉ s, s ‚äÜ A ‚ąß s.IsAPOfLengthWith (k : ‚Ąē‚ąě) a d
   234‚Üí
   235‚Üídef ContainsMonoAPofLength {őļ : Type} [Finite őļ] {M : Set őĪ}
   236‚Üí    (coloring : M ‚Üí őļ) (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
   237‚Üí  ‚ąÉ c : őļ, ‚ąÉ ap : Set M, ((¬∑.1) '' ap).IsAPOfLength k ‚ąß
   238‚Üí    ‚ąÄ m ‚ąą ap, coloring m = c
   239‚Üí
   240‚Üí/--
   241‚ÜíA function `f : őĪ ‚Üí ő≤` has a monotone `k`-term arithmetic progression if there exists an
   242‚Üíarithmetic progression `l` of length `k` in `őĪ` such that its image under `f` is sorted.
   243‚Üí-/
   244‚Üídef HasMonotoneAP {ő≤ : Type*} [Preorder ő≤] (f : őĪ ‚Üí ő≤) (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
   245‚Üí  ‚ąÉ l : List őĪ, l.IsAPOfLength k ‚ąß (l.map f).Pairwise (¬∑ < ¬∑)
   246‚Üí
   247‚Üí/--
   248‚ÜíDefine the largest possible size of a subset of a finset `s` that does not contain
   249‚Üíany non-trivial `k`-term arithmetic progression.
   250‚Üí-/
   251‚Üínoncomputable def Finset.maxAPFreeCard (k : ‚Ąē) (s : Finset őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
   252‚Üí  (s.powerset.filter fun t : Finset őĪ ‚Ü¶ (t : Set őĪ).IsAPOfLengthFree k).sup Finset.card
   253‚Üí

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 871
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/871](https://www.erdosproblems.com/871)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIf $A$ is an additive basis of order $2$ whose representation function tends to infinity,
    25‚Üícan $A$ always be partitioned into two disjoint additive bases of order $2$?
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[ErNa88] ErdŇĎs, P. and Nathanson, M.B., *Partitions of bases into disjoint unions of bases*,
    28‚ÜíJ. Number Theory (1988).
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[ErNa89] ErdŇĎs, P. and Nathanson, M.B., *Sets of natural numbers with no minimal asymptotic
    31‚Üíbases*, Proc. Amer. Math. Soc. (1989).
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen Classical Filter
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen scoped Topology
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace Erdos871
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- The sumset $A + A = \{a + b : a, b \in A\}$. -/
    41‚Üídef sumset (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    42‚Üí  {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą A, n = a + b}
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an additive basis of order $2$ if every sufficiently large
    45‚Üínatural number can be written as the sum of two elements of $A$. -/
    46‚Üídef IsAdditiveBasis2 (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n ‚Č• N‚āÄ, n ‚ąą sumset A
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- The representation function $r_A(n) = |\{a \in \{0, \ldots, n\} : a \in A \land n - a \in A\}|$,
    50‚Üíi.e., the number of ways to write $n$ as a sum of two elements of $A$. -/
    51‚Üínoncomputable def repCount (A : Set ‚Ąē) (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    52‚Üí  ((Finset.range (n + 1)).filter (fun a => a ‚ąą A ‚ąß (n - a) ‚ąą A)).card
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/--
    55‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 871 (DISPROVED) [ErNa88]:
    56‚Üí
    57‚ÜíLet $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ be an additive basis of order $2$, and suppose
    58‚Üí$1_A * 1_A(n) \to \infty$ as $n \to \infty$ (i.e., the number of representations
    59‚Üíof $n$ as a sum of two elements of $A$ tends to infinity). Can $A$ be partitioned
    60‚Üíinto two disjoint additive bases of order $2$?

    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 37
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/37](https://www.erdosproblems.com/37)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA lacunary set cannot be an essential component (a set that strictly increases the
    25‚ÜíSchnirelmann density of every set it is added to). Proved by Ruzsa [Ru87].
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Ru87] Ruzsa, I.Z., _Essential components_. Proc. London Math. Soc. (3) **54** (1987), 38‚Äď56.
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üíopen Classical
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üínamespace Erdos37
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/--
    35‚ÜíThe Schnirelmann density of a set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$, defined as
    36‚Üí$$d_s(A) = \inf_{n \geq 1} \frac{|A \cap \{1,\ldots,n\}|}{n}$$
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def schnirelmannDensity (A : Set ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    39‚Üí  ‚®Ö n : ‚Ąē+, (((Finset.Icc 1 (n : ‚Ąē)).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card : ‚ĄĚ) / ((n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ)
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/--
    42‚ÜíThe sumset $A + B = \{a + b \mid a \in A, b \in B\}$ for sets of natural numbers.
    43‚Üí-/
    44‚Üídef sumset (A B : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    45‚Üí  {n | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą B, n = a + b}
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/--
    48‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an *essential component* if $d_s(A + B) > d_s(B)$ for every
    49‚Üí$B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ with $0 < d_s(B) < 1$, where $d_s$ is the Schnirelmann density.
    50‚Üí-/
    51‚Üídef IsEssentialComponent (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    52‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (B : Set ‚Ąē), 0 < schnirelmannDensity B ‚Üí schnirelmannDensity B < 1 ‚Üí
    53‚Üí    schnirelmannDensity (sumset A B) > schnirelmannDensity B
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is *lacunary* if there exists $q > 1$ such that for any two
    57‚Üíconsecutive elements $a < b$ of $A$ (with no element of $A$ strictly between them),
    58‚Üíwe have $b \geq q \cdot a$.
    59‚Üí-/
    60‚Üídef IsLacunary (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    61‚Üí  ‚ąÉ (q : ‚ĄĚ), q > 1 ‚ąß ‚ąÄ (a b : ‚Ąē), a ‚ąą A ‚Üí b ‚ąą A ‚Üí a < b ‚Üí
    62‚Üí    (‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, ¬¨(a < c ‚ąß c < b)) ‚Üí (b : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• q * (a : ‚ĄĚ)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 37, proved by Ruzsa [Ru87].
    66‚ÜíA lacunary set cannot be an essential component.
    67‚ÜíRuzsa proved that if $A$ is an essential component then there exists some constant
    68‚Üí$c > 0$ such that $|A \cap \{1,\ldots,N\}| \geq (\log N)^{1+c}$ for all large $N$, which rules
    69‚Üíout lacunary sets (which satisfy $|A \cap \{1,\ldots,N\}| = O(\log N)$).
    70‚Üí-/
    71‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    72‚Üítheorem erdos_37 :
    73‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (A : Set ‚Ąē), IsLacunary A ‚Üí ¬¨IsEssentialComponent A := by
    74‚Üí  sorry
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üíend Erdos37
    77‚Üí

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 35
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/35](https://www.erdosproblems.com/35)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIf $B$ is an additive basis of order $k$ and $A$ has Schnirelmann density $\alpha$, then
    25‚Üí$d_s(A + B) \geq \alpha + \alpha(1-\alpha)/k$. Proved by Pl√ľnnecke [Pl70].
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Pl70] Pl√ľnnecke, H., _Eine zahlentheoretische Anwendung der Graphentheorie_. J. Reine Angew.
    28‚ÜíMath. 243 (1970), 171‚Äď183.
    29‚Üí-/
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üíopen Classical Finset BigOperators
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üínamespace Erdos35
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/-- The sumset $A + B$: the set of all $a + b$ with $a \in A$, $b \in B$. -/
    36‚Üídef sumset (A B : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē := {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą B, n = a + b}
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- Schnirelmann density of a set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$:
    39‚Üí$$d_s(A) = \inf_{N \geq 1} \frac{|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}|}{N}$$ -/
    40‚Üínoncomputable def schnirelmannDensity (A : Set ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    41‚Üí  sInf {x : ‚ĄĚ | ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, N ‚Č• 1 ‚ąß x = ((Icc 1 N).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card / (N : ‚ĄĚ)}
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- A set $B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an additive basis of order $k$ if every natural number
    44‚Üícan be written as a sum of exactly $k$ elements from $B$ (with repetition).
    45‚ÜíSince $0 \in B$ is assumed separately, "exactly $k$" is equivalent to "at most $k$". -/
    46‚Üídef IsAdditiveBasis (B : Set ‚Ąē) (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ f : Fin k ‚Üí ‚Ąē, (‚ąÄ i, f i ‚ąą B) ‚ąß ‚ąĎ i, f i = n
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/--
    50‚ÜíLet $B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ be an additive basis of order $k$ with $0 \in B$, and let
    51‚Üí$\alpha = d_s(A)$ be the Schnirelmann density of $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$. Then
    52‚Üí$$d_s(A + B) \geq \alpha + \frac{\alpha(1 - \alpha)}{k}.$$
    53‚Üí
    54‚ÜíThis was proved by Pl√ľnnecke [Pl70], who showed the stronger inequality
    55‚Üí$d_s(A + B) \geq \alpha^{1-1/k}$, as observed by Ruzsa.
    56‚Üí-/
    57‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    58‚Üítheorem erdos_35
    59‚Üí    (A B : Set ‚Ąē) (k : ‚Ąē) (hk : k ‚Č• 1)
    60‚Üí    (hB : IsAdditiveBasis B k) (h0 : (0 : ‚Ąē) ‚ąą B) :
    61‚Üí    let őĪ := schnirelmannDensity A
    62‚Üí    schnirelmannDensity (sumset A B) ‚Č• őĪ + őĪ * (1 - őĪ) / (k : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    63‚Üí  sorry
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üíend Erdos35
    66‚Üí

    29‚Üí/--

    38‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 11]
    39‚Üítheorem erdos_38 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    40‚Üí    ‚ąÉ B : Set ‚Ąē, ¬¨ B.IsAddBasis ‚ąß ‚ąÉ f : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ, (‚ąÄ őĪ, 0 < őĪ ‚Üí őĪ < 1 ‚Üí f őĪ > 0) ‚ąß
    41‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (A : Set ‚Ąē) (N : ‚Ąē),

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 31
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/31](https://www.erdosproblems.com/31)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíGiven any infinite set $A \subset \mathbb{N}$, there exists a set $B$ of natural density zero
    25‚Üísuch that $A + B$ contains all except finitely many natural numbers. Proved by Lorentz [Lo54].
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Lo54] Lorentz, G. G., _On a problem of additive number theory_. Proc. Amer. Math. Soc. 5 (1954), 838‚Äď840.
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üíopen scoped Classical
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üínamespace Erdos31
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/-- The sumset $A + B$: the set of all $a + b$ with $a \in A$, $b \in B$. -/
    35‚Üídef sumset (A B : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē := {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą B, n = a + b}
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- A set $B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ has natural density zero if
    38‚Üí    $|B \cap \{0, \ldots, N-1\}| / N \to 0$ as $N \to \infty$. -/
    39‚Üídef HasNaturalDensityZero (B : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    40‚Üí  ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, N ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    41‚Üí    (((Finset.range N).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą B)).card : ‚ĄĚ) / (N : ‚ĄĚ) < őĶ
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/--
    44‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 31** (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďStraus, proved by Lorentz [Lo54]):
    45‚Üí
    46‚ÜíGiven any infinite set $A \subset \mathbb{N}$, there exists a set $B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$
    47‚Üíof natural density $0$ such that $A + B$ contains all except finitely many natural numbers.
    48‚Üí-/
    49‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    50‚Üítheorem erdos_31 (A : Set ‚Ąē) (hA : A.Infinite) :
    51‚Üí    ‚ąÉ B : Set ‚Ąē, HasNaturalDensityZero B ‚ąß
    52‚Üí      Set.Finite {n : ‚Ąē | n ‚ąČ sumset A B} := by
    53‚Üí  sorry
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üíend Erdos31
    56‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1146
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíErdŇĎs asked whether the set of 3-smooth numbers {2^m ¬∑ 3^n : m, n ‚Č• 0} is an essential component
    23‚Üíwith respect to Schnirelmann density.
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1146](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1146)
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Va99] Ruzsa, I. Z., *Sumsets and structure*, Combinatorial Number Theory and Additive
    28‚ÜíGroup Theory (1999).
    29‚Üí-/
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üíopen Classical
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üínamespace Erdos1146
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/--
    36‚ÜíThe Schnirelmann density of a set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$, defined as
    37‚Üí$$d_s(A) = \inf_{n \geq 1} \frac{|A \cap \{1,\ldots,n\}|}{n}.$$
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üínoncomputable def schnirelmannDensity (A : Set ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    40‚Üí  ‚®Ö n : ‚Ąē+, (((Finset.Icc 1 (n : ‚Ąē)).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card : ‚ĄĚ) / ((n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ)
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/--
    43‚ÜíThe sumset $A + B = \{a + b \mid a \in A, b \in B\}$ for sets of natural numbers.
    44‚Üí-/
    45‚Üídef sumset (A B : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    46‚Üí  {n | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą B, n = a + b}
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/--
    49‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an essential component if $d_s(A + B) > d_s(B)$ for every
    50‚Üí$B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ with $0 < d_s(B) < 1$, where $d_s$ is the Schnirelmann density.
    51‚Üí-/
    52‚Üídef IsEssentialComponent (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    53‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (B : Set ‚Ąē), 0 < schnirelmannDensity B ‚Üí schnirelmannDensity B < 1 ‚Üí
    54‚Üí    schnirelmannDensity (sumset A B) > schnirelmannDensity B
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/--
    57‚ÜíThe set of 3-smooth numbers: $\{2^m \cdot 3^n \mid m, n \geq 0\}$.
    58‚Üí-/
    59‚Üídef smoothNumbers23 : Set ‚Ąē :=
    60‚Üí  {k | ‚ąÉ m n : ‚Ąē, k = 2 ^ m * 3 ^ n}
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/--
    63‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1146 [Va99, 1.19]:
    64‚ÜíIs the set $B = \{2^m \cdot 3^n : m, n \geq 0\}$ an essential component?
    65‚Üí
    66‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an essential component if $d_s(A + B) > d_s(B)$ for every
    67‚Üí$B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ with $0 < d_s(B) < 1$, where $d_s$ is the Schnirelmann density.
    68‚Üí
    69‚ÜíRuzsa states: "The simplest set with a chance to be an essential component is
    70‚Üíthe collection of numbers in the form $2^m \cdot 3^n$ and ErdŇĎs often asked whether
    71‚Üíit is an essential component or not."
    72‚Üí-/
    73‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 11]
    74‚Üítheorem erdos_1146 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ IsEssentialComponent smoothNumbers23 := by
    75‚Üí  sorry
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üíend Erdos1146
    78‚Üí

    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/432](https://www.erdosproblems.com/432)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíAsked by Straus, inspired by a problem of Ostmann (see Problem #431).
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí[ErGr80] ErdŇĎs, P. and Graham, R., *Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number
    27‚Üítheory*. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üíopen scoped Classical
    31‚Üíopen Filter

    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/--
    36‚ÜíThe upper density of $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$:
    37‚Üí$$d^*(A) = \limsup_{N \to \infty} \frac{|A \cap \{0, 1, \ldots, N-1\}|}{N}$$
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üínoncomputable def upperDensity (A : Set ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    40‚Üí  limsup (fun N : ‚Ąē => ((Finset.range N).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card / (N : ‚ĄĚ))
    41‚Üí    atTop
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/--
    44‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 432 [ErGr80, p.85]:
    45‚Üí
    46‚ÜíLet $A, B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ be two infinite sets. If all elements of the sumset
    47‚Üí$A + B = \{a + b \mid a \in A, b \in B\}$ are pairwise coprime, then $A + B$ has
    48‚Üízero upper density.
    49‚Üí-/
    50‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    51‚Üítheorem erdos_432
    52‚Üí    (A B : Set ‚Ąē) (hA : A.Infinite) (hB : B.Infinite)
    53‚Üí    (h_coprime : ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą Set.image2 (¬∑ + ¬∑) A B,
    54‚Üí      ‚ąÄ y ‚ąą Set.image2 (¬∑ + ¬∑) A B, x ‚Č† y ‚Üí Nat.Coprime x y) :
    55‚Üí    upperDensity (Set.image2 (¬∑ + ¬∑) A B) = 0 := by
    56‚Üí  sorry

    16‚Üíimport FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SetFamily.VCDim
    17‚Üíimport Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Action.Pointwise.Set.Basic
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# VC dimension in a group
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíThis file defines the Vapnik‚ÄďChervonenkis (aka VC) dimension of a set in a group, defined as the
    23‚ÜíVC dimension of its family of translates.
    24‚Üí
    25‚ÜíIt also defines the VC‚āô dimension, which has no set family analogue.
    26‚Üí-/
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üíopen scoped Pointwise
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üívariable {G : Type*} [CommGroup G] {A B : Set G} {n d d' : ‚Ąē}
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üívariable (A d) in
    33‚Üí/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VC dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    34‚Üí`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]` respectively such that
    35‚Üí`y s * x i ‚ąą A ‚ÜĒ i ‚ąą s` for all `i ‚ąą [d + 1]`, `s ‚äÜ [d + 1]`. -/
    36‚Üí@[to_additive
    37‚Üí/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VC dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    38‚Üí`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]` respectively such that
    39‚Üí`y s + x i ‚ąą A ‚ÜĒ i ‚ąą s` for all `i ‚ąą [d + 1]`, `s ‚äÜ [d + 1]`. -/]
    40‚Üídef HasMulVCDimAtMost : Prop :=
    41‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (x : Fin (d + 1) ‚Üí G) (y : Set (Fin (d + 1)) ‚Üí G), ¬¨ ‚ąÄ i s, y s * x i ‚ąą A ‚ÜĒ i ‚ąą s
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí@[to_additive]
    44‚Üílemma HasMulVCDimAtMost.mono (h : d ‚Č§ d') (hd : HasMulVCDimAtMost A d) :
    45‚Üí    HasMulVCDimAtMost A d' := by
    46‚Üí  rintro x y hxy
    47‚Üí  replace h : d + 1 ‚Č§ d' + 1 := by omega
    48‚Üí  exact hd (x ‚ąė Fin.castLE h) (y ‚ąė Set.image (Fin.castLE h)) fun i s ‚Ü¶ (hxy ..).trans <| by simp
